items.title: Zimbabwe's Mnangagwa signs law extending presidency to 2030

items.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
items.description: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz tanker attack raises tensions as Iran mourns Khamenei
items.description: A liquefied natural gas tanker caught fire after being struck by a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in one of the world's most critical shipping lanes. The attack comes as Iran mourns Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and nuclear talks with the United States remain stalled.

items.title: Milei leans on the caste to seek re-election
items.description: The arrival of Diego Santilli as Cabinet Chief signals a pragmatism that is both the result of a lack of trustworthy people in the ranks and Milei’s acknowledgment that he needs yet another dose of “caste” to align the means with the goals of his administration. Leer más

items.title: Experts urge caution as demand grows for AC in heatwave-hit U.K.
items.description: British households opt for AC after second heatwave of 2026 last month

items.title: Welcoming the Taliban? — Germany's new Afghanistan policy
items.description: To facilitate the deportation of criminals to Afghanistan, the German government is allowing Taliban diplomats into the country.

items.title: Argentina targets investment grade rating by 2031, Caputo says
items.description: Milei administration is targeting investment-grade status for Argentina by the end of a potential second term in 2031, announces minister. Leer más

items.title: Argentina rallies to beat Egypt 3-2 and reach the World Cup quarterfinals
items.description: Enzo Fernandez scored in stoppage time to give Argentina an incredible 3-2 win over Egypt and a spot in the World Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday. The defending champions had trailed 2-0 after goals from Yasser Ibrahim and and Mostafa Zico at Mercedes-Benz Stadium. But Argentina got two late goals in

items.title: Lionel Scaloni: 'I became a coach so I could experience these emotions'
items.description: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni hails his side's character after Tuesday's dramatic 3-2 comeback victory over Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Temasek’s net portfolio value grows 10.5% to S$518 billion; Mideast conflict dampens gains
items.description: Strong performance by Singapore-based listed companies and gains from key divestments boosted Temasek’s portfolio.

items.title: Mali: Sabotage plunges Bamako into water and power crisis
items.description: Large parts of Mali's capital have been left without electricity and running water after suspected sabotage damaged a key power transmission line. The disruption has deepened hardship for residents as insecurity and jihadist attacks continue to strain the country's fragile infrastructure.

items.title: Arrogance as state policy and the end of Manuel Adorni
items.description: Manuel Adorni's resignation, in which he accused the press of "media attacks," comes across as pretty pathetic from a spokesman and Cabinet chief who built his tenure on arrogance and who now stands accused of corruption. Leer más

items.title: What to know about NATO’s summit in Turkey as America steps back from its defence of Europe
items.description: President Donald Trump and NATO leaders are meeting in Turkey for a summit focused on the alliance’s future

items.title: Watch: Monsoon rains trigger deadly debris slip in Wayanad |Above the Fold | 07.07.2026
items.description: From Kerala to Indonesia, and from Syria to the FIFA World Cup, in this episode, we look at the deadly debris slip in Kerala, Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Indonesia, explosions in Syria during French President Emmanuel Macron’s trip, the Gujarat High Court’s ruling on the 2008 Ahmedabad blasts case, and more.

items.title: France, Mbappe play Morocco in FIFA World Cup quarterfinal: What we know
items.description: Key talking points as Kylian Mbappe leads France into a rematch after Les Bleus beat Morocco in the 2022 World Cup.

items.title: Brothers accused of killing stepfather outside primary school face court
items.description: DNA testing in the investigation of two brothers accused of murdering their stepfather outside a Melbourne primary school could take up to three months, a court hears.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Could football break away from FIFA and its World Cup?
items.description: FIFA’s actions at the World Cup, and closeness to Donald Trump, have put the organization under fire. Federations, fans, coaches and players have gone public with complaints, but is there a way out?

items.title: Far-right AfD reaffirms ambition to govern Germany
items.description: The AfD has strengthened its hardline leadership despite ongoing scrutiny from Germany's domestic intelligence agencies. Co-leader Alice Weidel says the far-right party is ready to govern after strong polling gains.

items.title: Iran’s economy faces long road to recovery as fragile truce tested
items.description: The devastation that industrial facilities have taken from two wars in a year might take years to reverse, say analysts.

items.title: U.S. launches ‘powerful’ strikes on Iran after Hormuz attacks
items.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry accused the United States of repeatedly violating the memorandum of understanding agreed between the two sides and threatened retaliation

items.title: Jehovah's Witnesses fight German state for Holocaust archive
items.description: The Jehovah's Witnesses have secured a partial victory in a dispute over a unique archive documenting the community's persecution by the Nazi regime. It comes after a new memorial to the victims was unveiled in Berlin.

items.title: U.S. charges Lawrence Bishnoi, Goldy Brar for Nijjar’s killing in Canada in 2023
items.description: The killing of Nijjar soured bilateral relations between India and Canada as then Prime Minister Justin Trudeau sought to link the government in New Delhi to the murder

items.title: France: Le Pen vows to run despite upheld graft conviction
items.description: After a court appeal rendered her eligible in theory to run while wearing an ankle tag, the far-right leader says she intends to stand next year. She repeated her plan to appeal again, saying the tag may not be needed.

items.title: Experts warn Trump is playing politics with US intelligence
items.description: Former officials warn that Trump's intelligence overhaul risks politicising national security.

items.title: Uruguay veteran Edison Cavani departs Boca Juniors
items.description: Uruguayan striker leaves Boca without having won a title and acknowledges he had "difficult moments" that prevented him from "leaving a mark" on the club. Leer más

items.title: 'We didn't want to go home' – Messi on Argentina's dramatic comeback
items.description: Skipper on his emotions following victory over Egypt: "It was a feeling of pure happiness and relief." Leer más

items.title: With SpaceX Starship, Japan's ispace provides ride-share to the moon
Millions mourn Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
items.description: Hundreds of thousands of mourners have gathered in Iran for the funeral ceremonies of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: UN Secretary-General calls for AI regulation
items.description: The Secretary-General of the United Nations has called for the creation of a global governance system to ensure that artificial intelligence serves humanity, warning against allowing the technology itself to "code our future by feel."

items.title: Australia's Ioneer signs MoUs with S Korea's KIND, Hyundai Engineering for lithium-boron project
US immigration officer shoots and kills man in Texas
items.description: Man, identified as Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, is latest to be killed by ICE officers since President Trump took power.

items.title: How Volkswagen's huge workforce became a liability
items.description: The struggling German carmaker plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs from its 630,000-strong workforce. How did VW's headcount get so bloated, and will the cuts be enough to survive the Chinese EV onslaught?

items.title: Bomb explosions injure 18 in Damascus during Macron's visit
items.description: As reports of the blasts came in, state television said the Syrian president had welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

items.title: French far-right chief Marine Le Pen cleared to run for President but with ankle tag
items.description: A Paris appeals court found Marine Le Pen guilty over the fake jobs scam at the European Parliament but reduced that sentence, banning her from office for 15 months as well as sentencing her to one year to be served with an electronic tag

items.title: Tinubu Inaugurates Presidential Working Group on National Policing Bill
items.description: President Tinubu said that while the constitutional amendment creates the framework for state police, the National Policing Bill would provide the legal structure for its implementation. The post Tinubu Inaugurates Presidential Working Group on National Policing Bill appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: At least 15 killed, 9 missing as storm batters China
items.description: Thunderstorms and strong winds swept through cities including Huangshi and Huanggang, killing eight people
